# hfprop

> HF propagation conditions for field operators. Check band conditions, solar indices, and get FreeDV mode recommendations from the terminal. Zero runtime dependencies. Works offline. Companion tool for ReticulumHF.

## Install

```bash
pip install .
```

Zero runtime dependencies. Python standard library only. Works on Raspberry Pi.

## Commands

```bash
hfprop now              # Current conditions at a glance
hfprop bands            # Band-by-band breakdown with FreeDV mode suggestions
hfprop solar            # Full solar/geomagnetic indices
hfprop path EM73 JN48   # Point-to-point path analysis between grid locators
hfprop watch            # Continuous monitoring (hourly refresh)
hfprop history          # Condition history from local SQLite database
hfprop serve            # Expose data over Reticulum mesh (requires reticulum extra)
hfprop query <hash>     # Query remote hfprop node over Reticulum
```

## Global flags

```bash
hfprop --grid EM73 now    # Location-aware (day/night/grayline detection)
hfprop --offline now      # Use cached data only (no network)
hfprop --json now         # JSON output for scripting
hfprop --no-color now     # Disable ANSI colors
```

## Features

- Solar indices: SFI, SSN, K-index, A-index, X-ray flux
- Band conditions: 80m through 10m, day/night columns
- Grid locator support: solar elevation, day/night/grayline detection
- Path analysis: great-circle distance, bearing, band recommendations for both endpoints
- FreeDV mode suggestions: DATAC1 (290bps), DATAC3 (124bps), DATAC4 (87bps) based on conditions
- Watch mode: continuous monitoring with change detection
- History: automatic SQLite logging, review past conditions
- Reticulum network service: share propagation data over mesh networks
- Offline mode: fetch once with internet, use cached data in the field

## Data sources

- HamQSL/N0NBH: solar indices + band conditions (hourly)
- NOAA SWPC: K-index, solar flux (minutes)

## Related

- [ReticulumHF](https://github.com/LFManifesto/ReticulumHF): HF data transport over Reticulum

## Author

Created by Heathen.
